---
title: "Codex Academic Workflow"
subtitle: "A Codex-first guide to planning, verification, review, and reproducible academic work"
author: "Pedro H. C. Sant'Anna"
date: "2026-04-08"
format:
  html:
    toc: true
    toc-depth: 3
    toc-location: left
    number-sections: true
    theme:
      - cosmo
      - custom.scss
    code-copy: true
    code-overflow: wrap
    highlight-style: github-dark
    smooth-scroll: true
    self-contained: true
    link-external-newwindow: true
    include-in-header:
      text: |
        <style>
        :not(pre) > code { background-color: rgba(185,151,91,0.1) !important; color: #8b6914 !important; padding: 0.15em 0.45em !important; border-radius: 3px !important; font-size: 0.87em !important; }
        pre code { background-color: transparent !important; color: inherit !important; padding: 0 !important; font-size: inherit !important; border-radius: 0 !important; }
        body:not(.floating):not(.docked) .page-columns.toc-left {
          grid-template-columns: [screen-start] 1.5em [screen-start-inset] 5fr [page-start] 35px [page-start-inset] minmax(0px,175px) [body-start-outset] 35px [body-start] 1.5em [body-content-start] minmax(450px,calc(1100px - 3em)) [body-content-end] 1.5em [body-end] 50px [body-end-outset] minmax(0px,200px) [page-end-inset] 35px [page-end] 5fr [screen-end-inset] 1.5em [screen-end] !important;
        }
        </style>
---

# Why This Branch Exists {#sec-why}

The original repository was built around Claude-specific runtime features: auto-loaded rules, slash-command skills, hook wiring, and Claude-only control primitives. Those ideas were useful, but the control plane was tied to one tool.

This branch rewrites that layer for Codex.

The migration keeps the useful parts:

- durable plans and session logs on disk
- verification before claiming completion
- explicit review passes by document type
- reusable scripts and templates
- support for LaTeX, Quarto, R, Python, MATLAB, and paper workflows

The migration changes the agent interface:

- `AGENTS.md` replaces `CLAUDE.md` as the primary instruction file
- explicit workflow docs replace auto-loaded `.claude/rules/*`
- plain-language requests replace slash-command skills
- Codex follows procedures directly instead of relying on Claude hooks

::: {.callout-note}
## Design Goal

Preserve the workflow logic, not the vendor-specific mechanics.
:::

# Quick Start {#sec-setup}

## Step 1: Clone the Repository

```bash
git clone https://github.com/YOUR_USERNAME/claude-code-my-workflow.git my-project
cd my-project
```

## Step 2: Open the Repo in Codex

Start with the root instruction file:

- `AGENTS.md`

For non-trivial work, also read the matching workflow docs:

- `docs/codex-workflows/plan-first.md`
- `docs/codex-workflows/orchestrator.md`
- `docs/codex-workflows/review-routing.md`
- `docs/codex-workflows/session-logging.md`
- `docs/codex-workflows/capabilities.md`

## Step 3: Use a Plain-Language Request

Instead of slash commands, ask directly:

- "compile this LaTeX file"
- "proofread this section"
- "review this paper"
- "translate this Beamer deck to Quarto"
- "validate the citations"
- "run the data analysis"

## Suggested Starter Prompt {#sec-first-session}

```text
I am starting to work on [PROJECT NAME] in this repo. [Describe the project in 2-3 sentences, including the file types and tools involved.]

Use the Codex workflow in this branch. Read AGENTS.md and the relevant files in docs/codex-workflows/, then adapt your work to this repository's conventions.

For non-trivial tasks, write a plan to quality_reports/plans/ before editing. Verify outputs before claiming completion. Use the correct review lenses for the files you touch.
```

# Core Workflow {#sec-system}

## Plan First

For work that is ambiguous, broad, or likely to touch multiple files:

1. read `MEMORY.md` if prior lessons matter
2. clarify ambiguity with a few targeted questions if needed
3. save the plan to `quality_reports/plans/YYYY-MM-DD_short-description.md`
4. execute only after the approach is clear

For migrations or vague requests, capture:

- `MUST`
- `SHOULD`
- `MAY`
- `CLEAR`
- `ASSUMED`
- `BLOCKED`

See `docs/codex-workflows/plan-first.md`.

## Implement, Verify, Review

The Codex branch treats orchestration as an explicit operating loop:

1. implement
2. verify
3. review
4. fix
5. re-verify
6. re-review if the fixes were material

This replaces the old hook-driven contractor mode.

See `docs/codex-workflows/orchestrator.md`.

## Keep Context on Disk

Conversation history is not durable enough for serious project state. This branch keeps working memory on disk:

| Purpose | Location |
| --- | --- |
| Persistent lessons | `MEMORY.md` |
| Plans | `quality_reports/plans/` |
| Session logs | `quality_reports/session_logs/` |
| Merge reports | `quality_reports/merges/` |

This lets Codex resume work from repository state rather than from fragile chat context.

# Review Model {#sec-review}

Codex can still behave like a specialist reviewer, but the specialization is now described as explicit lenses rather than Claude runtime subagents.

## Review Routing

| Document Type | Path Pattern | Lenses |
| --- | --- | --- |
| Cover letters | `Letters/**/*.tex` | proofreader, domain, narrative, letter-quality |
| Slides | `Slides/**/*.tex`, `Quarto/**/*.qmd` | proofreader, narrative, domain, layout |
| Research papers | `master_supporting_docs/**/*.tex` | proofreader, derivation, figure-consistency, theory, narrative |
| Code and analysis | `**/*.py`, `**/*.m`, `**/*.R` | correctness, structure, reproducibility |
| Documentation | `*.md`, `docs/**/*.md` | proofreader, command-check, link-check |

See `docs/codex-workflows/review-routing.md`.

## Findings First

For review requests, the preferred response shape is:

1. findings ordered by severity
2. open questions or assumptions
3. short summary only after the findings

That keeps review output operational instead of chatty.

# Quality Gates {#sec-quality}

The inherited thresholds are:

| Score | Gate | Meaning |
| --- | --- | --- |
| 90 | Commit | production-ready |
| 95 | External review | ready for collaborators or supervisors |
| 98 | Send or deploy | ready to publish or submit |
| 60 | Exploration | acceptable for fast prototype work |

## Non-Negotiable Rules

- Do not self-score after fixes. Re-check the current files.
- Do not claim verification unless you actually ran the relevant command or inspection.
- Stop qualitative review if compilation or execution is failing.

# Capability Map {#sec-capabilities}

The old skill catalog is now a plain-language capability map.

| User Intent | Codex Behavior |
| --- | --- |
| Compile LaTeX | run the correct LaTeX command, inspect the output, report blockers |
| Proofread | use the proofreader lens and save findings if needed |
| Review a paper | use findings-first output with theory and derivation checks |
| Validate citations | cross-check citation keys against bibliography files |
| Translate to Quarto | preserve source-of-truth and verify the rendered output |
| Run data analysis | execute code, regenerate outputs, report reproducibility status |

See `docs/codex-workflows/capabilities.md`.

# Repository Structure {#sec-structure}

| Path | Role |
| --- | --- |
| `AGENTS.md` | primary Codex instructions |
| `docs/codex-workflows/` | operating procedures |
| `scripts/` | reusable automation |
| `templates/` | plan, log, and report templates |
| `quality_reports/` | plans, logs, reviews, merge reports |
| `master_supporting_docs/` | papers, models, outputs, nested repositories |
| `.claude/` | legacy reference material, not authoritative for this branch |

# What Stayed Legacy {#sec-legacy}

The repository still contains the original `.claude/` directory. It is intentionally kept for reference, but Codex should not rely on:

- `.claude/settings.json` hook wiring
- `.claude/skills/*/SKILL.md` slash-command semantics
- `.claude/rules/*` auto-loading behavior
- Claude-only control concepts like `EnterPlanMode` or `AskUserQuestion`

If a future cleanup is needed, those assets can be archived or removed. For now, they remain as migration reference material.

# Recommended Migration Pattern {#sec-migration-pattern}

If you are adapting another Claude-oriented repo to Codex, the sequence used here is a reasonable default:

1. create `AGENTS.md`
2. extract workflow logic into explicit docs
3. replace slash commands with plain-language task recipes
4. keep scripts and templates intact
5. downgrade the old Claude files to reference status
6. regenerate public docs so the repo does not advertise the wrong runtime model

# Appendix: Active Codex Files {#sec-appendix}

## Primary Files

| File | Purpose |
| --- | --- |
| `AGENTS.md` | Codex behavior contract |
| `README.md` | Codex-first repo overview |
| `docs/codex-migration.md` | migration map |

## Workflow Documents

| File | Purpose |
| --- | --- |
| `docs/codex-workflows/plan-first.md` | planning and ambiguity handling |
| `docs/codex-workflows/orchestrator.md` | implement-verify-review loop |
| `docs/codex-workflows/review-routing.md` | review lenses by file type |
| `docs/codex-workflows/session-logging.md` | logging and merge reporting |
| `docs/codex-workflows/capabilities.md` | task recipes in place of slash commands |

## Legacy Reference

| Path | Status |
| --- | --- |
| `.claude/` | retained for comparison and migration reference |
| `CLAUDE.md` | retained as a pointer to `AGENTS.md` |
